description: Fix security-related inaccuracies in the MCP server reference page. The /ws-info endpoint no longer returns wsSecret, the unauthenticated /health response was reduced to just {status: 'ok'}, and plugin.uninstall is a Request (not a Notification).
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix /ws-info and /health endpoint documentation
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the MCP server reference to accurately describe the /ws-info and /health API responses so that I don't rely on stale response schemas.
acceptanceCriteria: The /ws-info response example shows only `{ "wsUrl": "ws://localhost:9515/ws" }` with no wsSecret field
The /ws-info description text does NOT mention 'auth secret' or 'wsSecret' — it says the endpoint returns the WebSocket URL for extension bootstrap
The explanation paragraph after the /ws-info response block does NOT mention wsSecret — it only describes the wsUrl field
The unauthenticated /health response example shows only `{ "status": "ok" }` with no version or extensionConnected fields
The text describing unauthenticated /health accurately states that only the status field is returned
The HTTP Endpoints summary table row for /ws-info says 'Returns WebSocket URL for extension bootstrap' (no mention of secret)
The lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx

Three changes needed:

1. Lines 223-228: The /ws-info response JSON block shows `{ "wsUrl": "...", "wsSecret": "<secret>" }`. Remove the wsSecret field. The actual handler at platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts:243 returns `Response.json({ wsUrl })` — only wsUrl, no wsSecret.

2. Line 230: The paragraph says 'The wsSecret field is included only when authentication is configured.' Remove this sentence entirely. Replace with something like: 'The extension uses this URL to establish the WebSocket connection. The wsUrl is derived from the server's host and always points to the /ws endpoint.'

3. Line 37 (HTTP Endpoints table): The /ws-info description says 'Returns WebSocket URL and auth secret for extension bootstrap.' Change to 'Returns WebSocket URL for extension bootstrap.' Remove 'and auth secret'.

4. Lines 130-135: The unauthenticated /health response shows `{ "status": "ok", "version": "0.0.4", "extensionConnected": true }`. Change to `{ "status": "ok" }`. The actual handler at platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts:256 returns `Response.json({ status: 'ok' })` — only status field for unauthenticated requests.

5. Update text around line 126-127 to reflect that unauthenticated requests receive only the status field.

userStories.title: Fix plugin.uninstall protocol classification
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want the WebSocket protocol table to correctly classify plugin.uninstall as a Request so that protocol implementations handle it correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: The WebSocket protocol table row for plugin.uninstall shows 'Request' in the Type column (not 'Notification')
The description for plugin.uninstall accurately states it expects a response from the extension
The lastUpdated frontmatter is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx

Lines 386-389: The table row for `plugin.uninstall` shows Type as 'Notification'. Change to 'Request'.

The actual code at platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts:582-586 sends `plugin.uninstall` with `id: getNextRequestId()`, making it a JSON-RPC Request that expects a response, not a fire-and-forget Notification.

Note: the lastUpdated may already be set to 2026-02-28 from US-001 if both stories edit the same file. That's fine — just ensure it's set.
